query_normalized: predicting the impact of cold work on recrystallization kinetics in aluminum alloys materials science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) because it investigates the effects of severe plastic deformation (the mechanism of cold work) on the microstructural evolution (grain refinement and dislocation contribution) of aluminum alloys, which are the fundamental physical variables governing recrystallization kinetics, even though the paper focuses on the resulting strength behavior rather than the kinetics rate itself.
